topic: Field decay >=28 nT (SAA regional vs global bait-and-switch)
change_summary: Since c2 (2026-04-07), detail_verdict_text grew 517% (286 -> 1765) and detail_evidence grew 54% (1876 -> 2891). c2 recommended major_rewrite; it was applied. Re-reviewing the rewritten text for new defects.
our_argument_summary: The Misleading verdict still holds. The rewrite successfully incorporates the c2 recommendations: the dome’s own "alone" quote is in detail_evidence, the batch-registration pattern (WIN-035 through WIN-039 all 2026-03-06) is documented, the parameter-as-prediction circularity is emphasized, and the Timestamp-Error cross-reference is wired up. However, the rewrite introduced a triplication bug (same sentence repeated three times at the end of detail_evidence) and a duplication of the Domain 8 / Tsumeb statement, and the code_analysis continues to misattribute the 28 nT threshold to Domain 8 when the live Domain 8 output shows a 77 nT/yr PRED=OBS identity check; WIN-037 is actually tracked as the static W011 confirmation, not as a live monitor.py domain.

relevant_files: raw-text/02-wins.txt lines 43-45 — PROS-007 prediction text and confirmation text
raw-text/02-wins.txt line 125 — WIN-037 entry (static W011 confirmation)
raw-text/03-predictions.txt line 44 — PRED-012 field decay >=28 nT/year by 2030 (PENDING)
raw-text/03-predictions.txt line 68 — W011 mapping: Field decay >=28 nT since Mar 2025 -> ~30 nT confirmed -> WIN-037
raw-text/05-model.txt lines 42, 75 — Field decay rate listed as core model parameter
raw-text/08-live-power.txt lines 228-230 — Domain: SAA Decay Rate Predicted 77.0 nT/yr = Observed 77.0 nT/yr, 20% tolerance, Source INTERMAGNET TTB annual means
raw-text/08-live-power.txt lines 289-294 — our own notes documenting SAA 77.0=77.0 as a PRED=OBS identity check with 20% tolerance gaming
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: WIN-037 maps to PROS-007 and to the static W011 entry. The confirmation text is hardcoded in the WINs file: "NOAA WMM2025 / INTERMAGNET confirms SAA region alone dropped ~30 nT since January 2025. Exceeds threshold." There is no separate monitor.py domain that actively recomputes the 28 nT / 12-month threshold against live data. Domain 8 in raw-text/08-live-power.txt is labeled "SAA Decay Rate" and reports Predicted 77.0 nT/yr = Observed 77.0 nT/yr (identity match) with 20% tolerance, not a 28 nT/yr threshold comparison. The c2 review conflated these two monitoring artifacts; the current detail_evidence inherits that conflation.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Hardcoded. The confirmation ("~30 nT dropped") is static text in raw-text/02-wins.txt, not a live computation. The Domain 8 live panel shows a PRED=OBS identity at 77.0 nT/yr (a different number, and a different logic). The 28 nT / 12-month claim is a one-shot static confirmation.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: The word "global" in the PROS-007 claim is not backed by any global computation. If the SAA region (fastest-declining area on Earth) drops >=28 nT over any 12-month window, the dome claims "global" confirmation. There is no failure path.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: Our detail_evidence says "The dome’s monitoring code (Domain 8) checks only Tsumeb, confirming the test was never truly global" and later "The dome’s monitoring code (Domain 8) checks only a single INTERMAGNET station (Tsumeb) in the SAA core, confirming the test was never global." Both sentences attribute the 28 nT check to Domain 8. But the live Domain 8 output (raw-text/08-live-power.txt lines 228-230) is labeled "SAA Decay Rate" and tests a 77.0 nT/yr PRED=OBS identity, not a 28 nT/yr threshold. The 28 nT WIN-037 claim is tracked as the static W011 confirmation (raw-text/02-wins.txt line 125), not as a live Domain 8 check. The "checks only Tsumeb" geography is right (TTB = Tsumeb), but the numerical threshold and the mechanism (static confirmation vs live domain) are misattributed.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: The W011 confirmation ("~30 nT dropped") is static text in raw-text/02-wins.txt line 45 and in the WIN list line 125. There is no live recomputation path. The separate Domain 8 "SAA Decay Rate" panel uses 77.0 nT/yr as a Predicted=Observed identity (also hardcoded). Either way, "hardcoded" is correct — there is no genuine live fetch that could make this WIN fail.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: SAA regional decay of 20-35 nT/yr is tracked continuously by NOAA WMM, ESA Swarm, INTERMAGNET, and IGRF. The mechanism (reversed-flux patches at the core-mantle boundary) is mainstream geomagnetism. The dome relabels this as "aetheric field decay" but predicts the same numerical value from the same INTERMAGNET data.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: PROS-007 registered 2026-03-06, confirmed by 2026-03-12 (a 6-day turnaround). WMM2025 released December 2024; INTERMAGNET TTB data is continuously public. Batch pattern: WIN-035 through WIN-039 all registered on 2026-03-06, consistent with a same-day scan of available data. The SAA has declined at 20-35 nT/yr for over a decade, so a >=28 nT / 12-month threshold was effectively pre-satisfied at registration.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: raw-text/05-model.txt line 42 lists "Field decay rate: >=28 nT/year" as a core model parameter. No formula connects disc_radius (20,015 km), firmament_height (9,086 km), or any dome geometry to 28 nT/yr. The value is observationally adopted, not geometrically derived.

holes_found.description: Sentence triplication bug in detail_evidence. The sentence "WIN-042 makes the mathematically equivalent claim framed as an annualized rate (>=28 nT/yr) rather than a 12-month cumulative threshold — the same measurement expressed twice does not constitute two independent confirmations." appears THREE times in a row at the end of detail_evidence (verified by indexOf count = 3). This is a patch-application defect that every reader will see — a screenshot-dunk-worthy bug for a dome defender.
holes_found.recommendation: Collapse the three copies into a single instance. The sentence itself is good content; the repetition is mechanical. Likely caused by a patch-script appending without checking for pre-existing occurrence. Add a repetition-detection check to the patch pipeline.

holes_found.description: Near-duplicate statement in detail_evidence. Both of these sentences appear in the same paragraph, four sentences apart: (1) "The dome’s monitoring code (Domain 8) checks only Tsumeb, confirming the test was never truly global." (2) "The dome’s monitoring code (Domain 8) checks only a single INTERMAGNET station (Tsumeb) in the SAA core, confirming the test was never global." These restate the same claim with slightly different wording. Reader will feel the prose is sloppy.
holes_found.recommendation: Keep one version — the second is more specific ("single INTERMAGNET station ... in the SAA core") and more informative, so drop the first.

holes_found.description: Code-analysis misattribution. detail_evidence asserts that the dome’s monitoring code (Domain 8) checks only Tsumeb against a >=28 nT/yr threshold. In the actual raw-text/08-live-power.txt (lines 228-230), Domain 8 is labeled "SAA Decay Rate" and reports Predicted 77.0 nT/yr = Observed 77.0 nT/yr with 20% tolerance. WIN-037’s 28 nT claim is tracked as the static W011 confirmation (raw-text/02-wins.txt line 125 / 03-predictions.txt line 68), not by a live Domain 8 threshold comparison. The "checks only Tsumeb" geography is correct — TTB = Tsumeb — but attributing the 28 nT threshold to a Domain 8 check is inaccurate.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) rewrite to "the dome’s live monitoring panel includes a Tsumeb-only SAA decay domain, confirming the dome only looks at the SAA core" (dropping the 28 nT threshold attribution to Domain 8); or (b) distinguish clearly: "WIN-037’s confirmation is a static W011 entry (~30 nT confirmed); the dome’s live Domain 8 panel reinforces the SAA-only mindset by also monitoring only Tsumeb." Option (b) is stronger because it exposes both the static confirmation and the live panel’s SAA-only geography.

holes_found.description: Time-window mismatch unflagged. PROS-007 predicts decay "in the 12 months since March 2025" (March 2025 -> March 2026). The dome’s confirmation says "SAA region alone dropped ~30 nT since January 2025" — a ~14-month window, not 12 months. Annualized, 30 nT / 14 months * 12 months = 25.7 nT/yr, BELOW the 28 nT/yr threshold. The dome’s own confirmation, read precisely, does not meet its own rate threshold when properly annualized.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a one-sentence kicker: "The confirmation itself uses a 14-month window (January 2025 - March 2026) to report the ~30 nT drop, not the 12-month window specified in the prediction. Annualized, that is 25.7 nT/yr — BELOW the dome’s own 28 nT/yr threshold." This is a specific, testable inconsistency the dome cannot hand-wave.

holes_found.description: Claim-wording ambiguity between cumulative and rate. The summary-table "claim" is "Field decay >=28 nT" (cumulative). The model parameter (raw-text/05-model.txt line 42) is "Field decay rate: >=28 nT/year" (rate). These are numerically equivalent only over a 12-month window. WIN-042’s duplication works precisely because the claim-text and parameter-text collapse the rate/cumulative distinction. We call out the duplication with WIN-042 but never explicitly connect it to the claim/parameter wording equivalence.
holes_found.recommendation: One-sentence clarification: "Because 28 nT/year (rate) and 28 nT over 12 months (cumulative) are numerically identical under a constant-rate assumption, WIN-037 and WIN-042 collapse into a single claim with two labels." This makes the duplication argument airtight.

stronger_arguments.description: A dome defender’s strongest objection to our "regional-vs-global" argument is that 28 nT/yr is within rounding distance of both the SAA value (~30 nT/yr) and the global-average we cite (~15 nT/yr). We can preempt this by citing the IGRF-13 total-field secular variation map: the strongest surface decay is clustered over South America / South Atlantic at ~100 nT/yr; the Atlantic Ocean is ~50 nT/yr; much of Asia and the Pacific shows ZERO or positive decay (field intensification). A "global" claim cannot be equivalent to ">=28 nT/yr" when roughly half the globe shows no decay at all.
sources: IGRF-13 / IGRF-14 secular variation map (Alken et al., Earth Planets and Space, 2021)

stronger_arguments.description: Our text does not use the dome’s own Domain 8 live-audit panel (77.0 = 77.0 PRED=OBS identity at 20% tolerance) as ammunition. The dome is publishing, on the same site, a "SAA Decay Rate" live check where predicted = observed identically — a textbook tolerance-gamed confirmation. We should leverage this: "The dome’s own live audit panel shows SAA Decay Rate predicted 77.0 nT/yr = observed 77.0 nT/yr with 20% tolerance — the predicted value was set to match the observed value, not derived independently. The WIN-037 confirmation is of the same class."
sources: raw-text/08-live-power.txt lines 228-230 (Domain: SAA Decay Rate panel)
raw-text/08-live-power.txt lines 289-294 (notes documenting PRED=OBS identity pattern)

kernel_of_truth.description: The SAA IS decaying faster than the global average. The ~28-30 nT/yr figure at Tsumeb is real and well-measured. The dome correctly identifies this as a notable geomagnetic phenomenon.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Standard geomagnetism attributes SAA decay to reversed-flux patches at the core-mantle boundary (MHD plus paleomagnetic reconstructions). The dome offers no alternative physical mechanism, no derivation from dome geometry, and no numerical prediction that differs from a WMM2025 extrapolation. Accurately observing a well-known phenomenon that standard physics already predicts is not a model confirmation — it is a data lookup.

advocate_mode.best_defense: I am a dome defender steelmanning PROS-007. Your critique rests on three legs: (1) regional-not-global, (2) 28 nT is a model input parameter, (3) post-hoc registration timing. I will answer each. On (1): the word "global" in PROS-007 is imprecise natural language — what the dome is testing is "the planet’s magnetic field, as most commonly measured," and the INTERMAGNET network with Tsumeb as a keystone station IS the standard proxy for global field health. Scientists publish "dipole moment" as a global number derived largely from the same stations; your 15 nT/yr figure is a dipole-moment translation, not a surface-field measurement. If the dome is wrong to use SAA Tsumeb, then IGRF is equally wrong. On (2): every physical model has parameters. Newton’s G is a parameter; that doesn’t make measuring gravitational attraction circular. The dome’s 28 nT/yr is a parameter fitted to historical data, and PROS-007 tests whether the fitted trend continues — a legitimate forecast. On (3): a 6-day gap between registration and confirmation is not evidence of post-hoc, it is evidence that the confirming datum (WMM2025) was available and the dome promptly logged it.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Add a short paragraph to detail_evidence explicitly rebutting the "parameter is fitted, test is whether trend continues" framing. The key disanalogy with Newton’s G: a fitted parameter becomes a real forecast ONLY IF the model commits to a derivation from that parameter to an independently measurable quantity. The dome’s 28 nT/yr parameter is not used to derive anything — it IS the prediction, tested against itself. Newton’s G enables predictions about orbital periods, tidal heights, satellite trajectories — hundreds of cross-checks. The dome’s 28 nT/yr enables exactly one prediction (the 28 nT/yr trend continues). That is a parameter-as-prediction tautology, not a fitted-parameter-with-discriminating-consequences. Compact text: "Fitting a parameter is scientifically legitimate when that parameter then produces discriminating predictions about different quantities. The dome’s 28 nT/yr decay parameter produces exactly one prediction: that the 28 nT/yr decay continues. That is not forecasting — it is trend-extrapolation labeled as forecasting." On the "registered with 2 weeks remaining" defense: the registration date (2026-03-06) is at the END of the 12-month window, when ~99% of the decay had already accumulated; the observation was essentially complete. Note this explicitly.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: No contradictions. WIN-004 and WIN-005 attribute SAA behavior to MHD core dynamics; WIN-037 attributes SAA decay to "reversed-flux patches at the core-mantle boundary" — same mechanism, consistent descriptions. WIN-035 (SAA African cell <21,795 nT) and WIN-036 (NP deviation) carry the "Non-discriminating: dome extrapolates same station trends as WMM2025" finding, consistent with WIN-037’s structural critique. WIN-042 is explicitly flagged as the duplicate and cross-references WIN-037 for full analysis. The batch-registration date (2026-03-06 across WIN-035..WIN-039) is mentioned in WIN-037’s detail_evidence and is consistent across WINs. Minor wording variations are fine — no defender can compile a "your review contradicts itself" attack from this set.

claims_checked: 5% per century global dipole decline -> ~15 nT/yr global-average translation
28 nT / 12-month cumulative vs 28 nT/year rate equivalence
Time-window mismatch: 12 months claimed vs 14 months in confirmation (Jan 2025 -> Mar 2026)
Triplication count of the WIN-042 reference sentence in detail_evidence
Citation resolution for DOI 10.1186/s40623-015-0228-9 (Thebault et al. 2015)
quantitative_verification.computations: (1) 0.05 * 30,000 nT / 100 years = 15 nT/yr — verified our cited number. (2) 28 nT/year rate over 12 months = 28 nT cumulative — equivalent under constant-rate assumption, validates WIN-037 <=> WIN-042 duplication argument. (3) 30 nT over 14 months = 30 * 12/14 = 25.7 nT/yr implied rate — BELOW the 28 nT/yr threshold when properly annualized. (4) indexOf count returned 3 for the WIN-042 sentence — triplication confirmed. (5) DOI 10.1186/s40623-015-0228-9 resolves to "International Geomagnetic Reference Field: The 12th generation" by Thebault, Finlay, Beggan et al., 2015, Earth Planets and Space — citation valid.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: Surprising sub-finding from (3): if the dome’s confirmation uses a 14-month window to report 30 nT, the SAA decay rate implied is 25.7 nT/yr — BELOW the 28 nT/yr threshold when properly annualized. The dome’s own confirmation, read precisely, does not meet its own rate threshold. This should be surfaced in our text as a ready-made self-refutation in addition to the existing regional-vs-global argument.

deeper_analysis: This WIN is a textbook example of three stacked post-hoc tricks: (a) batch-registering predictions on a single day against already-published data; (b) conflating rate and cumulative so the same observation can be counted as two WINs; (c) using "global" in the prediction and "regional" in the confirmation. The rewrite for c3 successfully addresses most c2 concerns but introduced prose defects (triplication + near-duplicate Domain 8 sentence) and inherited a code-analysis misattribution (Domain 8 threshold confusion). The verdict "Misleading" is exactly right and should not change. Recommended action minor_edit covers: (1) collapse the triplication; (2) dedupe the Domain 8 sentence; (3) rewrite or split the Domain 8 attribution; (4) add the 14-month window / 25.7 nT/yr implied rate as a kicker; (5) preempt the "parameter-as-prediction is legitimate like Newton’s G" defense. None of these are verdict-changing; all of them tighten the argument.

citations_verified: DOI 10.1186/s40623-015-0228-9 -> Thebault et al. 2015, IGRF-12, Earth Planets and Space — verified via WebFetch
citations_unchecked: https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/products/world-magnetic-model — domain exists, not deep-checked for 20-35 nT/yr at Tsumeb claim
